finding: Unicode 17.0.0 Scripts.txt uses long Script values while ScriptExtensions.txt uses four-letter aliases; PropertyValueAliases.txt is the canonical namespace bridge. Restriction-level and mixed-script logic should consume one normalized four-letter namespace and reject unknown or duplicate aliases fail-closed.

primary_sources.status: official Unicode 17.0.0 data; inspected through primary-source endpoint
official Unicode 17.0.0 alias data; inspected through primary-source endpoint

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

evidence_strength: deterministic fixture-tested implementation with official-source format grounding
uncertainty: The complete PropertyValueAliases.txt and ScriptExtensions.txt bytes were not retained or digest-pinned in this slice.
Full Unicode 17.0.0 conformance was not executed in repository CI.
Normalization does not establish identifier safety or restriction level.
falsification_route: Provide a valid Unicode 17.0.0 Script alias record rejected by the parser.
Provide malformed, duplicate, or unknown aliases accepted by the parser.
Show that equivalent long and short Script names normalize to different four-letter aliases.
Run the parser against authenticated full Unicode 17.0.0 PropertyValueAliases.txt and demonstrate a mismatch.
privacy_boundary: Contains only public standards metadata, code paths, test outcomes, and a digest of public Unicode data; no private chat, health, credentials, or identifying personal material.
